| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Type** | `React.forwardRef` component |
| **Props (`ButtonProps`)** | Extends `React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>` + `VariantProps<buttonVariants>` |
| **`variant`** | `default` \| `destructive` \| `outline` \| `secondary` \| `ghost` \| `link` *(default: `"default"`)* |
| **`size`** | `default` \| `sm` \| `lg` \| `icon` *(default: `"default"`)* |
| **`asChild`** | `boolean` — renders a Radix UI `<Slot>` instead of `<button>` |
| **`isLoading`** | `boolean` — shows a `<Spinner>` and hides the icon |
| **`icon`** | `React.ReactNode` — prepends an icon inside a `<span>` |
| **Styling** | Driven by `cva` (class-variance-authority) via `buttonVariants` |

**File:** `components/post_view/post_list/post_list.tsx`, **line 157**

```ts
export default class PostList extends React.PureComponent<Props, State>
```

This is the **main orchestrator** component. Its responsibilities:
- Loads posts on mount/channel change via `loadUnreads`, `loadPostsAround`, `syncPostsInChannel`, or `loadLatestPosts`
- Manages `loadingOlderPosts`, `loadingNewerPosts`, and `autoRetryEnable` state
- Handles auto-retry logic (up to `MAX_NUMBER_OF_AUTO_RETRIES = 3`) and pagination guard (`MAX_EXTRA_PAGES_LOADED = 10`)
- In `render()`, it shows a `<LoadingScreen>` when no posts are available, otherwise delegates rendering to `<VirtPostList>` (the virtualized list)
- Connected to Redux via `components/post_view/post_list/index.tsx`, which wires up state (post chunks, formatted IDs, timestamps) and action creators

---

#### 2. `PostList` (alias `VirtPostList`) — Virtualized renderer
**File:** `components/post_view/post_list_virtualized/post_list_virtualized.tsx`, **line 151**

```ts
export default class PostList extends React.PureComponent<Props, State>
```

This is the **virtualized rendering engine** (imported elsewhere as `VirtPostList`). It handles:
- Rendering individual rows via `<PostListRow>`
- Scroll management, scroll position correction, and "at bottom" detection
- New messages separator, channel intro, toast notifications
- Overscan, slicing, and dynamic list styles for a `react-window`-style virtual list

In `mapStateToProps` (inside `makeMapStateToProps`), the return value is:

`postIds` is assigned from `chunk.order`:

```ts
    atLatestPost = Boolean(chunk.recent);
    atOldestPost = Boolean(chunk.oldest);

```

### Step 2 — Where `chunk` is determined (3-branch conditional)

`chunk` is a `PostOrderBlock` object that comes from one of **three selectors** depending on view state:

```ts
if (focusedPostId && focusedPost !== undefined) {
    // ① Permalink / focused view
    chunk = getPostsChunkAroundPost(state, focusedPostId, channelId);

} else if (unreadChunkTimeStamp && !shouldStartFromBottomWhenUnread) {
    // ② Unread messages view
    chunk = getUnreadPostsChunk(state, channelId, unreadChunkTimeStamp);

} else {
    // ③ Default / most-recent view
    chunk = getRecentPostsChunkInChannel(state, channelId);
}
```

| Branch | Selector | Source |
|--------|----------|--------|
| ① Permalink | `makeGetPostsChunkAroundPost()` (memoized) | `packages/mattermost-redux/src/selectors/entities/posts.ts:134` |
| ② Unread chunk | `getUnreadPostsChunk` | `packages/mattermost-redux/src/selectors/entities/posts.ts:649` |
| ③ Recent/default | `getRecentPostsChunkInChannel` | `packages/mattermost-redux/src/selectors/entities/posts.ts:571` |

All three selectors read from **`state.entities.posts.postsInChannel[channelId]`** — the Redux store's post-order cache — and return a `PostOrderBlock` whose `.order` array becomes `postListIds`.

```
Redux Store: state.entities.posts.postsInChannel[channelId]
```

| `hasInaccessiblePosts` | `boolean` *(required)* | Whether the channel has posts the user can't access (limited views). |
| `actions.loadPostsAround` | `(channelId, focusedPostId) => Promise<void>` | Loads posts around a specific post (permalink view). |
| `actions.loadUnreads` | `(channelId) => Promise<void>` | Loads unread posts on first visit. |
| `actions.loadPosts` | `(parameters) => Promise<LoadPostsReturnValue>` | Loads posts before/after a given post ID. |
| `actions.checkAndSetMobileView` | `() => Promise<void>` | Checks/sets mobile view state. |
| `actions.syncPostsInChannel` | `(channelId, since, prefetch) => Promise<void>` | Syncs posts since a timestamp. |
| `actions.loadLatestPosts` | `(channelId) => Promise<void>` | Loads the latest posts when no timestamp is available. |
| `actions.markChannelAsViewed` | `(channelId) => void` | Marks the channel as viewed. |
| `actions.markChannelAsRead` | `(channelId) => void` | Marks the channel as read. |

### 2. Where `postListIds` Originates — Full Trace

The `PostList` component rendered in the UI is actually the **Redux-connected version** exported from `index.tsx`. The `postListIds` prop is **never passed directly from the parent JSX** — it is injected entirely by Redux's `mapStateToProps`.

#### Step 1 — Parent Component (`post_view.tsx`)

`PostView` renders the connected `<PostList>` and passes only these JSX props:
```tsx
<PostList
    unreadChunkTimeStamp={this.state.unreadChunkTimeStamp}
    channelId={this.props.channelId}
    changeUnreadChunkTimeStamp={this.changeUnreadChunkTimeStamp}
    shouldStartFromBottomWhenUnread={this.state.shouldStartFromBottomWhenUnread}
    toggleShouldStartFromBottomWhenUnread={this.toggleShouldStartFromBottomWhenUnread}
    focusedPostId={this.props.focusedPostId}
/>
```
👉 **`postListIds` is NOT passed here at all.** It is injected by Redux.

---

#### Step 2 — Redux Connector (`index.tsx` → `makeMapStateToProps`)

Inside `makeMapStateToProps`, the logic to derive `postListIds` (called `postIds` locally) is:

```ts
// 1. Determine which chunk of posts to use:
if (focusedPostId && focusedPost !== undefined) {
    chunk = getPostsChunkAroundPost(state, focusedPostId, channelId);    // Permalink view
} else if (unreadChunkTimeStamp && !shouldStartFromBottomWhenUnread) {
    chunk = getUnreadPostsChunk(state, channelId, unreadChunkTimeStamp); // Unread view
} else {
    chunk = getRecentPostsChunkInChannel(state, channelId);              // Default: latest posts
}

// 2. Extract the ordered array of post IDs from the chunk:
if (chunk) {
    postIds = chunk.order;   // ← This becomes postListIds
    atLatestPost = Boolean(chunk.recent);
    atOldestPost = Boolean(chunk.oldest);
}

// 3. Map it to the prop name:
return {
    postListIds: postIds,   // ← Assigned here
    ...
};
```

---

#### Step 3 — Redux State Origin

The `chunk` is a `PostOrderBlock` object read from the Redux store at:
```
state.entities.posts.postsInChannel[channelId]
```
Each `PostOrderBlock` has an `order` field — **an array of post IDs sorted newest-to-oldest**. The selector (`getRecentPostsChunkInChannel`) finds the block marked as `recent: true`:

```ts
// packages/mattermost-redux/src/selectors/entities/posts.ts
export function getRecentPostsChunkInChannel(state, channelId) {
    const postsForChannel = state.entities.posts.postsInChannel[channelId];
    if (!postsForChannel) return null;
    return postsForChannel.find((block) => block.recent); // finds the most recent chunk
}
```
The `.order` array of that block is the exact data that becomes `postListIds`.

---

### 🗺️ Summary Data Flow

```
Redux Store
  └─ state.entities.posts.postsInChannel[channelId]   ← populated by API actions (loadPosts, loadUnreads, etc.)
       └─ PostOrderBlock[] (array of chunks)
            └─ .order: string[]                        ← ordered post IDs (newest → oldest)

index.tsx (makeMapStateToProps)
  ├─ getRecentPostsChunkInChannel(state, channelId)    ← default path (latest posts)
  ├─ getUnreadPostsChunk(state, channelId, ts)         ← unread path
  ├─ getPostsChunkAroundPost(state, postId, channelId) ← permalink path
  └─ chunk.order → postIds → mapped as postListIds: postIds

post_view.tsx (parent)
  └─ <PostList channelId={...} unreadChunkTimeStamp={...} .../>
       ↑ postListIds NOT passed here; injected by Redux connect()

post_list.tsx (PostList component)
  └─ this.props.postListIds  ← received from Redux, used for load-more logic & passed to VirtPostList
```

In short: **`postListIds` originates from `chunk.order`** — the ordered array of post IDs within a `PostOrderBlock` read from `state.entities.posts.postsInChannel[channelId]` in the Redux store. Which specific chunk is selected depends on whether the user is in permalink mode, unread mode, or the default latest-posts mode — all resolved inside `makeMapStateToProps` in `index.tsx`.
